content.problemStatement:
A thief wants to rob houses arranged in a line. Each house has some amount of money. The thief cannot rob two consecutive houses as it triggers an alarm. Find the maximum amount of money the thief can steal.


Input Format

First line contains a single integer NNN denoting the total number of houses
Second line contains NNN space-separated integers denoting the amount of money in each house


Output Format

Print a single integer representing the maximum amount of loot possible

Subtasks

1≤1 \leq 1≤ nums.length ≤100 \leq 100≤100
0≤0 \leq 0≤ nums[i] ≤400 \leq 400≤400

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Best way: Rob houses with money 1 + 3 + 5 = 9
Cannot rob consecutive houses as it triggers alarm

Explanation:
Best way: Rob houses with money 2 + 9 = 11
Cannot take 7 as it would be consecutive with either 2 or 9
